income_thresholds.description: Annual household income must be below Rs 3 lakh. BPL families are automatically eligible. Delhi opted OUT of PM-JAY; this is the state's alternative health coverage.

exclusion_rules.description: Only residents of Delhi with valid Delhi voter ID or residential proof are eligible
Must have Delhi voter ID or valid Delhi residential address proof. Non-Delhi residents cannot avail this scheme.
Families with annual income above Rs 3 lakh are not eligible
Families covered under CGHS, ESIC, or employer-provided insurance are excluded
Income tax paying individuals and their families are excluded

family_criteria.family_definition: Family as per ration card or voter ID address. All family members at the same Delhi address are covered.

covered_procedures: Hospitalization expenses at Delhi govt hospitals
excluded_procedures: Treatment at private hospitals (unless referred by govt hospital)

required_documents.name: Delhi Voter ID
required_documents.notes: Must prove Delhi residency. Voter ID is preferred. Ration card or utility bill with Delhi address also accepted.

required_documents.name: Income Certificate / BPL Card
required_documents.notes: Income certificate from SDM office or BPL card to prove income below Rs 3 lakh

required_documents.name: Hospital Referral Letter
required_documents.notes: Referral from Delhi govt hospital may be needed for treatment at certain empanelled facilities

enrollment_channels: Delhi government hospital Medical Social Worker (MSW) office
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Visit nearest Delhi government hospital when hospitalization is needed
Step 2: Meet the Medical Social Worker (MSW) at the hospital
Step 3: Submit Aadhaar, Delhi voter ID/ration card, and income certificate/BPL card
Step 4: MSW verifies income and residency documents
Step 5: If eligible, treatment cost is covered under Delhi Arogya Kosh up to Rs 5 lakh
Step 6: For treatments beyond government hospital capacity, referral to empanelled private facility may be arranged
Step 7: No pre-enrollment needed — apply at time of hospitalization with required documents
description_for_embedding: Delhi Arogya Kosh (DAK) is Delhi's primary health coverage scheme, created because Delhi opted OUT of PM-JAY. Provides up to Rs 5 lakh per family for hospitalization at Delhi government hospitals. Targeted at BPL families and those with annual income below Rs 3 lakh. Requires Delhi voter ID or residence proof and income certificate. Unlike PM-JAY, enrollment is primarily at the point of hospitalization through the Medical Social Worker at government hospitals. Delhi residents who are not covered by CGHS, ESIC, or employer insurance can avail this scheme. Portal: dgehs.delhi.gov.in.
